chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD CP No. D- 2470 of 2015 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 27.01.2016 1. For Orders on Office Objection 2. For Katcha Peshi 3. For Hearing of MA 11604/15 4. For Orders on MA 779/16 Petitioner present in person Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro Addl.A.G. The petitioner is aggrieved by the order dated 27.5.2015 whereby respondent No.3, on the recommendation of the Commissioner Hyderabad Division, Hyderabad has imposed a ban upon all kinds of revenue transactions in Taluka Sujawal, Mirpur Bathoro, Shah Bundar and Jati in revenue limits of District Sujawal till further orders. In response to the notice, respondent No.3 has filed comments stating therein that the ban was imposed upon the recommendation of the Commissioner, Hyderabad and thereafter Director E&I Board of Revenue Sindh along with Deputy Director (PRC) were appointed as Inquiry officer for conducting inquiry into the matter. The said committee after conducting a detailed inquiry has submitted its report along with the recommendations to lift the subject ban. It is further stated by respondent No.3 that recommendations of that committee along with proposal have been sent to the Commissioner, Hyderabad through letter dated 21.10.2015 for his comments which are still awaited. After going through the said comments of respondent No.3, we are of the view that the purpose of the petitioner would be well served, if we issue directions to the respondent No.4, Commissioner Hyderabad Division, Hyderabad to furnish his comments within a period of 20 days on the said letter, whereafter respondent No.3 / Senior Member board of Revenue shall in the light of those comments / views decide the fate of the subject ban within 10 days.

It is further stated by respondent No.3 that recommendations of that committee along with proposal have been sent to the Commissioner, Hyderabad through letter dated 21.10.2015 for his comments which are still awaited. After going through the said comments of respondent No.3, we are of the view that the purpose of the petitioner would be well served, if we issue directions to the respondent No.4, Commissioner Hyderabad Division, Hyderabad to furnish his comments within a period of 20 days on the said letter, whereafter respondent No.3 / Senior Member board of Revenue shall in the light of those comments / views decide the fate of the subject ban within 10 days. With these observations, this petition is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE *Karar/-
